description: LangChain initialize_agent and old OpenAI import
code:
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent
from langchain.llms import OpenAI
agent = initialize_agent(tools=[], llm=OpenAI())

known_issues.description: Moved from langchain.llms to langchain_openai in 0.2.0

notes: Most common LangChain hallucination — every pre-2024 tutorial uses this pattern

code:
from langchain.chains import LLMChain
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate

template = PromptTemplate(input_variables=['product'], template='What is a good name for {product}?')
chain = LLMChain(llm=llm, prompt=template)
result = chain.run('socks')

known_issues.description: Removed in 0.3.0 — replaced by LCEL pipe syntax

notes: LLMChain was the most-used chain type and appears in hundreds of tutorials

code:
from langchain.chat_models import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferMemory

chat = ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4', temperature=0)
memory = ConversationBufferMemory()

known_issues.description: Moved from langchain.chat_models to langchain_openai in 0.2.0
Moved from langchain.memory to langchain_community.memory in 0.2.0

notes: Double wrong-import case — both classes moved to different packages

description: LangChain full agent with all old patterns
code:
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType
from langchain.llms import OpenAI
from langchain.chains import LLMChain
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferMemory

llm = OpenAI(temperature=0)
memory = ConversationBufferMemory()
agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=[],
    llm=llm,
    agent=AgentType.ZERO_SHOT_REACT_DESCRIPTION,
    memory=memory
)
source: Composite of multiple LangChain migration issues
notes: Stress test — 4 issues in one snippet

description: LangChain agent with tools — old pattern
code:
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, load_tools
from langchain.llms import OpenAI

llm = OpenAI(temperature=0)
tools = load_tools(['serpapi', 'llm-math'], llm=llm)
agent = initialize_agent(tools, llm, agent='zero-shot-react-description')
notes: Classic tutorial pattern that every LLM memorized

code:

    model='gpt-4',

notes: Multiple v0 patterns in one script

known_issues.description: Removed in v2 — replaced by field_validator

source: Pydantic v1 to v2 migration guide
notes: @validator is the most commonly hallucinated Pydantic pattern

known_issues.description: Removed in v2 — replaced by model_validator

notes: root_validator replaced by model_validator(mode='before'|'after')

code:
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Item(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float

item = Item(name='Widget', price=9.99)
data = item.dict()
json_str = item.json()
notes: Common in any code that serializes Pydantic models

code:
from pydantic import BaseModel

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: str

data = {'name': 'Alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'}
user = User.parse_obj(data)
fields = User.__fields__
notes: parse_obj and __fields__ both removed in same v2 release

code:
import pandas as pd

df1 = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2], 'B': [3, 4]})
df2 = pd.DataFrame({'A': [5], 'B': [6]})
df3 = df1.append(df2, ignore_index=True)
notes: Single most broken pandas pattern in AI-generated code

code:
import pandas as pd
from pandas import Int64Index

idx = Int64Index([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
df = pd.DataFrame({'value': [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]}, index=idx)

known_issues.description: Removed in 2.0.0 — use pd.Index with dtype

notes: NumericIndex types all removed in same release

code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2], 'B': [3, 4]})
for col, series in df.iteritems():
    print(f'{col}: {series.sum()}')
notes: iteritems was deprecated in 1.5.0 and removed in 2.0.0

code:
import pandas as pd
from pandas import Int64Index

df1 = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2]}, index=Int64Index([10, 20]))
df2 = pd.DataFrame({'A': [3]})
df3 = df1.append(df2)
for col, series in df3.iteritems():
    print(col)
notes: Multiple deprecated patterns in one data pipeline

code:

from langchain.llms import OpenAI

llm_predictor = LLMPredictor(llm=OpenAI(temperature=0))
service_context = ServiceContext.from_defaults(llm_predictor=llm_predictor)

known_issues.description: Removed in 0.10.0 — replaced by Settings global config

notes: Cross-library failure — both LlamaIndex AND LangChain patterns are wrong

code:
from llama_index import GPTSimpleVectorIndex, ServiceContext, LLMPredictor, SimpleDirectoryReader

llm = LLMPredictor()
service_context = ServiceContext.from_defaults(llm_predictor=llm)
docs = SimpleDirectoryReader('./data').load_data()
index = GPTSimpleVectorIndex.from_documents(docs, service_context=service_context)
notes: 4 issues on a single import line

description: LlamaIndex old import for SimpleDirectoryReader only
code:

known_issues.description: Module path changed to llama_index.core in 0.10.0

notes: Subtle — class exists but import path is wrong

code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

agent = Agent(role='researcher', goal='research', backstory='expert')
task = Task(description='research AI trends', agent=agent)
crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
result = task.output
notes: Task output API completely restructured

code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process

researcher = Agent(role='researcher', goal='find information', backstory='expert researcher')
writer = Agent(role='writer', goal='write articles', backstory='expert writer')

task1 = Task(description='research topic', agent=researcher)
task2 = Task(description='write article', agent=writer)

crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, writer], tasks=[task1, task2], process=Process.sequential)

known_issues.description: allow_delegation default changed from True to False in 0.51.0 — must pass explicitly
allow_delegation default changed from True to False in 0.51.0

notes: Silent behavior change — code runs but delegates differently

code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

agent = Agent(role='analyst', goal='analyze', backstory='data expert')
task = Task(description='analyze data', agent=agent)
crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
crew.kickoff()
print(task.output)

known_issues.description: Removed in 0.28.0 — use crew.kickoff() return value

notes: Both delegation and output patterns wrong

code:
import numpy as np

arr = np.array([1, 0, 1], dtype=np.bool)
idx = np.array([1, 2, 3], dtype=np.int)

known_issues.description: np.bool removed in NumPy 2.0 — use bool or np.bool_
np.int removed in NumPy 2.0 — use int or np.int_

notes: Most common NumPy 2.0 breakage

code:
import numpy as np

x = np.zeros(10, dtype=np.float)
y = np.zeros(10, dtype=np.complex)
z = np.ones(5, dtype=np.object)

known_issues.description: np.float removed in NumPy 2.0 — use float or np.float64
np.complex removed in NumPy 2.0 — use complex or np.complex128
np.object removed in NumPy 2.0 — use object or np.object_

notes: Three dtype aliases removed in same release

code:
import numpy as np

names = np.array(['alice', 'bob'], dtype=np.str)
print(names)

known_issues.description: np.str removed in NumPy 2.0 — use str or np.str_

notes: np.str was alias for built-in str

code:
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///test.db')
with Session(engine) as session:
    users = session.query(User).filter(User.age > 18).all()

known_issues.description: Legacy Query API removed in 2.0 — use select() constructs

notes: session.query() was the primary ORM pattern for 10+ years

code:
from sqlalchemy import create_engine

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///test.db')
result = engine.execute('SELECT * FROM users')
for row in result:
    print(row)

known_issues.description: Removed in 2.0 — use connection context manager

notes: Connectionless execution was the most common pre-2.0 pattern

code:
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, create_engine
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)

known_issues.description: Removed in 2.0 — use DeclarativeBase class from sqlalchemy.orm

notes: declarative_base() was the standard ORM base pattern

code:

code:
from langgraph.graph import MessageGraph, END

graph = MessageGraph()
graph.add_node('chatbot', chatbot_fn)
graph.set_entry_point('chatbot')
graph.add_edge('chatbot', END)
app = graph.compile()

known_issues.description: Removed in 0.2.0 — use StateGraph with MessagesState

notes: MessageGraph was the primary graph type in LangGraph 0.1.x

code:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.checkpoint import MemorySaver

graph = StateGraph(dict)
graph.add_node('agent', agent_fn)
graph.set_entry_point('agent')
graph.add_edge('agent', END)
app = graph.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver())

known_issues.description: MemorySaver import path changed — now langgraph.checkpoint.memory

notes: Import path moved deeper into checkpoint submodule

code:
import pinecone
import numpy as np

pinecone.init(api_key='xxx', environment='us-east1-gcp')
pinecone.create_index('embeddings', dimension=768)
index = pinecone.Index('embeddings')
vectors = [(f'id_{i}', np.random.rand(768).tolist()) for i in range(100)]
index.upsert(vectors=vectors)
notes: Full Pinecone pipeline with all v2 patterns

code:

client = chromadb.Client()
collection = client.create_collection('my_collection')
collection.add(documents=['doc1', 'doc2'], ids=['id1', 'id2'])

known_issues.description: Generic Client removed in 0.4.0 — use EphemeralClient, PersistentClient, or HttpClient

notes: Must now specify storage backend explicitly

code:

from chromadb.config import Settings

client = chromadb.Client(Settings(
    chroma_db_impl='duckdb+parquet',
    persist_directory='./chroma_data',
    anonymized_telemetry=False
))
notes: Old Settings-based configuration completely changed

code:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, Dropout

model = Sequential([
    Dense(128, activation='relu', input_shape=(784,)),
    Dropout(0.2),
    Dense(10, activation='softmax')
])

known_issues.description: Keras 3.0 is standalone — use from keras.models import Sequential

notes: tensorflow.keras coupling removed in Keras 3.0

code:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

model = Sequential([Dense(10, input_shape=(5,))])
model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='mse')
model.fit_generator(data_generator, steps_per_epoch=100, epochs=10)

known_issues.description: Removed in Keras 3.0 — use model.fit which accepts generators

notes: Two Keras issues — wrong import path and removed method

code:
from transformers import TrainingArguments, Trainer

training_args = TrainingArguments(
    output_dir='./results',
    per_gpu_train_batch_size=16,
    num_train_epochs=3,
    learning_rate=5e-5
)
notes: per_gpu renamed to per_device to support non-GPU accelerators

description: Transformers pipeline with wrong model-task match
code:
from transformers import pipeline, AutoModelForCausalLM

model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('gpt2', device_map='auto')
classifier = pipeline('text-generation', model='bert-base-uncased')

known_issues.description: device_map='auto' requires pip install accelerate — missing dependency
BERT is not a text-generation model — use 'fill-mask' task or a causal LM

notes: Common LLM hallucination — mixing up model architectures

code:

from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

model = RandomForestClassifier()
joblib.dump(model, 'model.pkl')

known_issues.description: Removed in sklearn 0.23 — use import joblib directly

notes: joblib is now standalone — removed from sklearn.externals

code:
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()

@app.on_event('startup')
async def startup_event():
    print('Starting up...')

@app.on_event('shutdown')
async def shutdown_event():
    print('Shutting down...')

known_issues.description: on_event deprecated in 0.93.0 — use lifespan context manager

notes: on_event still works but generates deprecation warning

code:
import tensorflow as tf

x = tf.placeholder(tf.float32, shape=[None, 784])
W = tf.Variable(tf.zeros([784, 10]))
y = tf.matmul(x, W)

sess = tf.Session()
sess.run(tf.global_variables_initializer())
result = sess.run(y, feed_dict={x: data})

known_issues.description: Removed in TF 2.0 — use tf.function or Keras Input layers
Removed in TF 2.0 — eager execution is default

notes: Classic TF1 pattern still generated by LLMs trained on pre-2020 data
